description: Fallen wood and food scraps can 'morume' (decompose) at different rates and under different conditions. The verb describes breakdown through biological or chemical processes. 'mukesi' (spoil) says that a perishable thing is no longer fit for its intended use; decomposition is the material process itself.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', keep nasal airflow, and round into middle-height back 'o'. Tap or trill 'r' at the alveolar ridge and raise the back of the tongue into stressed rounded 'u'. Close the lips again for 'm', then finish with middle-height front 'e'.
usage_notes: The material is the subject of this intransitive verb. Imperfective 'si morume' presents active decomposition or rotting; perfective 'ki morume' presents a bounded completed breakdown. Any judgement about smell, usefulness, or safety belongs in its own clause.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Decomposition keeps a material's afterlife visible. Conditions determine whether it returns nutrients or leaves harmful residues.

semantic_domains.nature: biological or chemical breakdown within ecological material cycles
semantic_domains.physical: matter changing into different constituent forms over time
